sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Nothing has changed after 5 clicks. This is very puzzling. Let me reconsider the entire problem.
Maybe the board is rendered differently than I think. The image shows what appears to be a smaller grid - maybe the actual interactive elements are much smaller than the 6x6 blocks I've been assuming.
Looking at the image again, I see:
- 4 distinct 3x3 grids
- Each grid cell appears to be a single colored square in the image
- The center cell of each grid has a small pattern (white/grey/red)
- The bottom-right grid has a grey border
Maybe the actual grid is much smaller - like 21x21 or something - and the 64x64 representation is just a scaled-up version. But the segmentation shows 6x6 blocks...
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the issue is that I need to click on the small center elements within the center cells, not the large colored blocks. The center cells have small 2x2 sub-elements.
Looking at the top-left grid's center:
- Row 10-11: g2 W2 g2 (grey, white, grey)
- Row 12-13: W2 R2 g2 (white, red, grey)  
- Row 14-15: g2 W4 (grey, white)
The red 2x2 is at rows 12-13, cols 14-15. I already tried clicking (12, 14).
Let me try clicking on the white parts instead. The white 2x2 at rows 10-11, cols 14-15.
Or maybe I should try clicking on the grey frame pieces more carefully.
Actually, let me try a completely different approach. Let me click on the very center of each grid - the red dot in the center cell.
For the top-left grid, the red 2x2 is at rows 12-13, cols 14-15. Center would be (12.5, 14.5).
I tried (12, 14). Let me try (13, 15) instead.
Or maybe I need to click on the grey L-shaped corner pieces of the frame. Let me try clicking on the bottom-right corner of the frame.
Actually, let me try something completely different. Let me click on the charcoal (c) area inside the grey frame. That's the dark background inside the frame.
From the ascii, rows 34-35 show: B32g2c26g2B2
So at rows 34-35, cols 34-59 are charcoal (c). The charcoal area is the dark background inside the grey frame.
Let me try clicking there.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the charcoal (dark grey) background inside the grey frame
# From ascii: rows 34-35, cols 34-59 are charcoal
# Also try clicking on the white sub-element in center cells

# Try clicking on charcoal background inside frame
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 34, 'col': 34}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
